name: Z3 Theorem Prover (Microsoft)

performance.notes: SAT/SMT solving is NP-hard in general; simple constraints fast; complex problems timeout

agent_readiness.notes: State-of-the-art SMT (Satisfiability Modulo Theories) solver from Microsoft Research. Formal verification, constraint solving, program analysis. Supports integers, reals, bitvectors, arrays, strings, nonlinear arithmetic. Python, C, C++, .NET, Java bindings. Used in security research (binary analysis, vulnerability finding), program synthesis, formal methods. AI planning and scheduling. Model checking. Bug finding in smart contracts (Manticore, Mythril use Z3). Growing use in AI reasoning systems.

gotchas: SAT/SMT is computationally hard — problems can be undecidable
Steep learning curve (formal methods background helpful)
Timeout tuning required for production use
best_when: Security research (smart contract analysis, binary analysis)
AI planning and scheduling with hard constraints
avoid_when: Problems with complex nonlinear constraints (often timeout)
summary: Z3 is Microsoft Research's state-of-the-art SMT solver. Handles constraint satisfaction over integers, reals, bitvectors, arrays, and strings. Used in formal verification, security research (smart contract analysis, binary analysis), program synthesis, and AI planning. Python bindings. Powerful but computationally hard — problems can timeout. Steep learning curve; domain expertise recommended.
